goal: Land the G4-architecture feed (call hierarchy incoming/outgoing + type hierarchy supertypes) in `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` so P1-W5-F06 flips to passes=true and the downstream W5 chain (P1-W5-F08 integration suite) can begin. Mirrors the WO-0016 shape: a single new module `call_hierarchy.rs` that writes into the §12.1 `entities` + `relations` tables via `KnowledgeGraph::execute_in_transaction`, driven by a `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` in module-root tests.
plan_summary: Add `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs` with three public async persist functions — `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming`, `persist_call_hierarchy_outgoing`, and `persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes` — each dispatching through the existing `DiagnosticsClient` (so `tokio::time::timeout` via `LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` is already enforced) and persisting the resulting hierarchy edges as rows in the §12.1 `entities` + `relations` tables. Each function opens **one** `KnowledgeGraph::execute_in_transaction` scope per invocation: inside the closure it raw-INSERTs a root `entities` row (kind projected from `lsp_types::SymbolKind`), then for each returned peer item it raw-INSERTs a peer `entities` row AND a `relations` row (`kind="calls"` for call edges, `kind="inherits"` for supertype edges) linking root ↔ peer. This mirrors WO-0016's `persist_diagnostics` pattern exactly — raw `INSERT INTO` through `tx.prepare` + `params!`, no dependency on any `upsert_entity` helper (WO-0020 territory), and cycle-free under DEC-0008. Tests live at module root as `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_<name>()` (DEC-0005 / frozen selector `call_hierarchy::`) with a `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` adapted from WO-0016's pattern and a real on-disk SQLite via `tempfile::TempDir` + `KnowledgeGraph::open`.
scope_in: crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — NEW module file. Follows the `quality_pipeline.rs` template exactly: head rustdoc block with §13.5 / §12.1 / §15.2 citations, `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` for the `CallHierarchyError` naming, `use` block, error enum, internal row structs, the three persist functions, then `#[cfg(test)]` submodules (`fake_serena_client`, `test_fixtures`), then module-root `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_*` declarations.

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — `pub enum CallHierarchyError` with `#[non_exhaustive]` and at minimum: `#[error("LSP dispatch failed: {0}")] Dispatch(#[from] DiagnosticsClientError)`, `#[error("non-file URI: {uri}")] NonFileUri { uri: String }`, `#[error("knowledge graph error")] KnowledgeGraph(#[from] KnowledgeGraphError)`. Derives `Debug, Error`.

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — internal `fn uri_to_file_path(uri: &Url) -> Result<String, CallHierarchyError>` helper (same signature as quality_pipeline.rs's private helper; do NOT pub-export, do NOT move to a shared module — each module keeps its own copy to preserve DEC-0008 cycle-freedom and avoid a shared-private-module slippery slope).

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — internal `fn symbol_kind_to_entity_kind(kind: lsp_types::SymbolKind) -> &'static str` mapping the LSP 3.17 `SymbolKind` values that Serena actually emits onto UCIL entity-kind strings. Required mapping: `Function`/`Method` → `"function"`; `Class`/`Interface`/`Struct`/`Enum` → `"type"`; `Constructor` → `"function"`; `Module`/`Namespace`/`Package` → `"module"`; `Variable`/`Constant`/`Field`/`Property` → `"variable"`; anything else → `"symbol"`. Document the mapping in the rustdoc as a table (WO-0016 severity-mapping precedent).

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — `pub async fn persist_call_hierarchy_incoming(client: &DiagnosticsClient, kg: &mut KnowledgeGraph, root_item: CallHierarchyItem, language: Language) -> Result<usize, CallHierarchyError>`. Behaviour: (1) open tracing span `ucil.lsp.persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` with attrs `root_uri`, `root_name`, `language`; (2) eagerly convert `root_item.uri` → file_path (surface `NonFileUri` before the LSP round-trip); (3) `client.call_hierarchy_incoming(root_item.clone()).await?`; (4) if vec is empty → `tracing::debug!("no incoming calls; skipping transaction")` and return `Ok(0)`; (5) inside **one** `kg.execute_in_transaction(|tx| { … })`, INSERT one root `entities` row using `symbol_kind_to_entity_kind(root_item.kind)` + `root_item.name` + `file_path` + `start_line = root_item.selection_range.start.line as i64` + `end_line = root_item.selection_range.end.line as i64`, capture `root_id = tx.last_insert_rowid()`; for each `CallHierarchyIncomingCall` INSERT a peer `entities` row from `call.from` and capture `peer_id`, then INSERT a `relations` row with `source_id = peer_id, target_id = root_id, kind = "calls", source_tool = "lsp:callHierarchy"`. (Incoming = peer calls root.) Return the count of relation rows inserted. `#[tracing::instrument(level="info", skip(client, kg, root_item), fields(root_name = %root_item.name))]`.

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — `pub async fn persist_call_hierarchy_outgoing(client: &DiagnosticsClient, kg: &mut KnowledgeGraph, root_item: CallHierarchyItem, language: Language) -> Result<usize, CallHierarchyError>`. Same pattern as incoming; dispatch is `client.call_hierarchy_outgoing`; direction is flipped: `source_id = root_id, target_id = peer_id` (outgoing = root calls peer); `kind = "calls"`; `source_tool = "lsp:callHierarchy"`. Span name `ucil.lsp.persist_call_hierarchy_outgoing`.

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — `pub async fn persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes(client: &DiagnosticsClient, kg: &mut KnowledgeGraph, root_item: TypeHierarchyItem, language: Language) -> Result<usize, CallHierarchyError>`. Dispatch is `client.type_hierarchy_supertypes(root_item.clone()).await?`; peer items are `TypeHierarchyItem`. Direction: `source_id = root_id, target_id = peer_id, kind = "inherits", source_tool = "lsp:typeHierarchy"`. Span name `ucil.lsp.persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes`.

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — Module-root `#[cfg(test)] mod fake_serena_client { … }` submodule housing a `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` that accepts three scripted maps (by root URI) for `call_hierarchy_incoming`, `call_hierarchy_outgoing`, `type_hierarchy_supertypes`; the `diagnostics(...)` method returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (unused by this module's tests). Pattern copied from `quality_pipeline.rs::fake_serena_client`. `FakeSerenaClient` implements UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait — this is NOT a mock of Serena's MCP wire format (DEC-0008 §Rationale).

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — Module-root `#[cfg(test)] mod test_fixtures { … }` submodule with: `fn open_kg() -> (TempDir, KnowledgeGraph)` (same shape as `quality_pipeline.rs::test_fixtures`), `fn make_call_hierarchy_item(name, uri, kind) -> CallHierarchyItem`, `fn make_type_hierarchy_item(name, uri, kind) -> TypeHierarchyItem`, and a helper that constructs the `DiagnosticsClient` around the `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient`.
crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs — Module-root tests (NOT inside `mod tests { }` — DEC-0005 / frozen selector `call_hierarchy::`). Exact test names:
* `fn test_symbol_kind_mapping_covers_serena_emitted_kinds()` — asserts the `symbol_kind_to_entity_kind` mapping for Function/Method/Class/Struct/Interface/Module/Variable/Unknown. Pure (no SQLite).

* `fn test_persist_call_hierarchy_incoming_writes_entities_and_relations()` — scripts 2 incoming callers for a root; calls `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming`; asserts returned count == 2; reads back from `relations` via `kg.conn().query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM relations WHERE kind = 'calls'", ...)` → 2; reads `entities` COUNT → 3 (1 root + 2 peers); checks `source_tool = 'lsp:callHierarchy'` on a sampled row; checks direction (incoming → peer is source, root is target) by joining entities on name.
* `fn test_persist_call_hierarchy_outgoing_flips_direction()` — scripts 3 outgoing callees; asserts direction flip (root is source, peer is target) via SQL join on name; count == 3.

* `fn test_persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes_writes_inherits_relations()` — scripts 2 supertypes for a class root; asserts relation rows have `kind = 'inherits'`, `source_tool = 'lsp:typeHierarchy'`, and direction (root is source, supertype is target); count == 2.

* `fn test_persist_empty_hierarchy_returns_zero()` — scripts an empty vec for incoming, outgoing, supertypes; each persist function returns `Ok(0)` and the `entities`/`relations` tables stay empty (COUNT = 0).

* `fn test_non_file_uri_surfaces_typed_error()` — constructs a `CallHierarchyItem` with a non-file URI (e.g. `untitled:Untitled-1`); asserts `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming` returns `Err(CallHierarchyError::NonFileUri { .. })` and the `entities`/`relations` tables stay empty (error fires BEFORE the transaction opens).

* `fn test_atomic_transaction_single_scope()` — scripts 3 peers; asserts that the single-scope invariant holds by reading `entities` + `relations` after the call and checking the COUNTs are exactly `(1 root + 3 peers = 4, 3 relations)`. This is the atomicity observable — if a writer split the inserts across multiple `execute_in_transaction` calls the COUNTs would still match, but the test also greps `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs` for exactly 3 occurrences of `execute_in_transaction(` (one per persist function) via `include_str!` at compile time. (The acceptance-criterion grep on the file provides the redundant external oracle.)

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/lib.rs — declare `pub mod call_hierarchy;` (after existing `pub mod quality_pipeline;`) and re-export `pub use call_hierarchy::{persist_call_hierarchy_incoming, persist_call_hierarchy_outgoing, persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes, CallHierarchyError};` under the existing re-export block (alphabetical-within-group, same style as the quality_pipeline re-exports at lib.rs:30-33).

Rustdoc on every new public item — `persist_call_hierarchy_incoming`, `persist_call_hierarchy_outgoing`, `persist_type_hierarchy_supertypes`, `CallHierarchyError`. Citations: master-plan §13.5 line 1436 (`Diag. bridge → G4 (Architecture): call hierarchy, type hierarchy`), §12.1 `relations`/`entities` schema, §15.2 tracing-span naming, DEC-0008 (cycle-free crate). Mirror the WO-0016 quality_pipeline.rs rustdoc style.
scope_out: No edits to `bridge.rs` / `diagnostics.rs` / `quality_pipeline.rs` / `types.rs` — those modules landed in WO-0014/0015/0016 and are frozen at this WO boundary. If a reader argues for a rustdoc fix or a shared helper extraction, STOP and write an ADR before touching them.

No edits to `crates/ucil-core/**` — no new `KnowledgeGraph` helpers, no `upsert_entity` / `upsert_relation` (that is WO-0020 territory for P1-W4-F02). Use raw `tx.prepare("INSERT INTO …")` + `params!` exactly like `persist_diagnostics` does. The §12.1 `entities` + `relations` DDL already ships all required columns (WO-0011).

No edits to `INIT_SQL` — the `entities` + `relations` + foreign-key + UNIQUE constraints are already in place at `knowledge_graph.rs:110-216`. If a column looks missing, STOP and write an ADR rather than editing DDL.
No edits to `crates/ucil-daemon/**` — DEC-0008 cycle-free invariant. The bridge remains daemon-independent.
No MCP tool plumbing — `find_definition` / `find_references` / `search_code` are P1-W4-F05 / P1-W5-F07 / P1-W5-F09 territory and owned by future daemon-side WOs.
No degraded-mode fallback LSP spawning — P1-W5-F07 owns `server_sharing::test_fallback_spawn`; this WO does NOT add a `server_sharing.rs` module or spawn any LSP subprocess.

No fixture-project end-to-end integration — `tests/fixtures/**` is forbidden to touch (root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract); P1-W5-F08 is the integration suite that runs against real fixture projects. This WO uses `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` for determinism.
No `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` wrapper — DEC-0005 / WO-0006/0007 regression. Module-root tests only; frozen selector `call_hierarchy::` is a path prefix.
No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` — tests open a real tempfile-backed SQLite db via `tempfile::TempDir::new()` + `KnowledgeGraph::open(...)` (WO-0016 `test_fixtures::open_kg` precedent).
No raw `std::sync::mpsc` / `std::thread::spawn` inside async — rust-style.md forbids it. The module is async-end-to-end.
No new workspace dep; no new `[workspace.members]` entry; no new crate.

No edits to `Cargo.toml` at workspace root (no new dep needed — `lsp-types`, `async-trait`, `rusqlite`, `tempfile`, `tokio` are all already pulled in by `ucil-lsp-diagnostics`; tracing macros via the existing `tracing` dep).
No edits to `ucil-build/feature-list.json` (root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract).

No new ADR — the §13.5 G4 feed semantics, §12.1 `entities`/`relations` schema, and DEC-0008 cycle-free rule are unambiguous for this WO. Raise a proposed ADR ONLY if a genuine spec ambiguity surfaces (e.g. multiple kind-vocabulary candidates with no clear winner).
acceptance: New module `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/call_hierarchy.rs` exists with the three persist functions + `CallHierarchyError`; `lib.rs` declares `pub mod call_hierarchy;` and re-exports the symbols.

`cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics 'call_hierarchy::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar` matches ≥ 7 tests (all the `test_*` declared above), all passing — the F06 acceptance selector (frozen).
`cargo clippy -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery — same regime WO-0014/0015/0016 established at `lib.rs:15-17`).
`cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links, no warnings, no errors).
`! grep -q 'ucil-daemon' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml` — DEC-0008 cycle-free invariant preserved.
Reality-check oracle: `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W5-F06` passes (stashing the new module causes `call_hierarchy::` to return 0 tests or compile-fails; popping restores).
No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection` or real Serena MCP wire format — tests open a real tempfile-backed SQLite db and drive UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait.
Module-root tests are flat — no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `call_hierarchy.rs` — per DEC-0005.

Every writer goes through `execute_in_transaction` — grep for `self.conn.execute(` or `kg.conn().execute(` in the new code returns nothing. Reads via `kg.conn().query_row(...)` / `.prepare(...)` are fine.

Exactly 3 occurrences of `execute_in_transaction(` in `call_hierarchy.rs` — one per persist function. More would indicate atomicity violation (splitting a logical unit across txns); fewer would indicate a persist function missing the BEGIN IMMEDIATE wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics 'call_hierarchy::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -cE '^\s+PASS.*call_hierarchy::' | awk '{exit ($1 < 7)}'
context_refs: master-plan:§13 lines 1374-1440 (Serena + LSP diagnostics bridge — §13.5 line 1436 is the G4 contract: `Diag. bridge → G4 (Architecture): call hierarchy, type hierarchy`)
master-plan:§13.3 lines 1412-1421 (the two async methods `call_hierarchy(file, line, col)` and `type_hierarchy(file, line, col)` — this WO's persist functions are the storage-bound companions)

master-plan:§12.1 lines 1127-1318 (`entities` + `relations` DDL — UNIQUE(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from), kind TEXT NOT NULL, source_tool/source_evidence/confidence — already landed by WO-0011)
master-plan:§11 line 1117 (PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; busy_timeout = 10000; BEGIN IMMEDIATE for every writer — enforced by `execute_in_transaction`)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 5 lines 1756-1765 (Week-5 bullet 6: `Wire diagnostics bridge → G4 architecture (call/type hierarchy enrichment)` — the exact P1-W5-F06 scope)

decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits (module-root tests — critical here; `call_hierarchy::` is the frozen selector prefix; `test_schema_creation` and `quality_pipeline::test_*` precedents)
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate (no per-WO cargo-mutants — reality-check.sh is the mutation oracle)

decision:DEC-0008-lsp-bridge-via-serena-mcp-channel (the trait-seam rationale: `SerenaClient` is UCIL's own trait; `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` in tests is NOT a mock of Serena's MCP wire; cycle-free crate — `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` MUST NOT depend on `ucil-daemon`)
work-order:WO-0016-diagnostics-quality-pipeline (P1-W5-F05 predecessor — landed `persist_diagnostics` — the EXACT template this WO mirrors for call/type hierarchy)
verification-report:WO-0016 (PASS precedent — 5 tests, 1023 lines, 5 files, pedantic+nursery clippy clean, reality-check documented limitation; same shape expected here)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror in libs; pedantic+nursery warn; tracing spans; no unwrap outside `#[cfg(test)]`; tokio timeouts on async IO — already enforced by DiagnosticsClient)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W5-F06`, `Work-order: WO-0023` trailers; push after every commit; no `--amend` after push; no force-push)

phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariant 1: no mocks of Serena MCP; invariant 6: no HTTP in Phase 1 — stdio/MCP only; invariant 8: SQLite opens in WAL mode with PRAGMA busy_timeout = 10000 — already enforced in WO-0011)
lessons_applied: Mirror WO-0016's exact structure — `quality_pipeline.rs` is the working template: head rustdoc with §/DEC citations, `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]`, error enum with `#[non_exhaustive]`, internal uri_to_file_path helper, one persist function per wire operation, `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` submodule, `test_fixtures` submodule, module-root `test_*` declarations. Deviation from this shape is a smell; justify in rustdoc if deviating.

Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 — the frozen selector `call_hierarchy::` is a path prefix; `test_schema_creation` at `knowledge_graph.rs:472` and the five `quality_pipeline::test_*` at `quality_pipeline.rs` are the shape. This was the WO-0006/0007 regression mode and the escalation 20260415-1856 record.

DEC-0008 cycle-free: NO edge from `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` to `ucil-daemon`. The existing `SerenaClient` trait in `diagnostics.rs` is the dependency-inversion seam. Do NOT add `ucil-daemon` to `Cargo.toml`. `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` is UCIL's own trait impl — structurally distinct from mocking Serena's MCP wire format.

No new `KnowledgeGraph` helpers — WO-0020 owns `upsert_entity`/`upsert_relation` for P1-W4-F02. This WO writes via raw `tx.prepare("INSERT INTO entities …")` + `tx.last_insert_rowid()` exactly as `persist_diagnostics` does for `quality_issues`. Keeps this WO unblocked by WO-0020's in-flight state and stays inside the crate boundary.

INIT_SQL stays byte-for-byte frozen (scope_out item) — the §12.1 `entities` + `relations` tables already ship all required columns (`kind`, `source_tool`, `source_evidence`, `confidence`, `t_valid_from`/`t_valid_to`, `start_line`/`end_line`) at `knowledge_graph.rs:110-138`. Do NOT add columns or indexes. If a column is missing, STOP and write an ADR.

All writers route through `execute_in_transaction` — the grep guard in `acceptance_criteria` requires exactly 3 occurrences (one per persist function). Direct `kg.conn().execute(...)` for writes is forbidden; reads via `kg.conn().query_row(...)` / `.prepare(...)` are fine.

Atomicity: each persist function opens EXACTLY ONE `execute_in_transaction` scope, inside which it inserts the root entity + all peer entities + all relations. Splitting a logical unit across transactions is forbidden — a partial peer-set with a dangling root entity breaks the G4 consumer (WO-0024 territory) invariants.

`source_tool` value frozen at `"lsp:callHierarchy"` for call edges and `"lsp:typeHierarchy"` for inherits edges — downstream fusion (phase-2+) looks for this exact prefix to attribute the edge to the diagnostics bridge. Mirror WO-0016's `format!("lsp:{}", diag.source.as_deref()…)` convention loosely (this WO's source is always the wire op name, not a per-language server).

Direction semantics — incoming calls = `relations(source_id=peer, target_id=root, kind='calls')` (peer calls root); outgoing calls = `relations(source_id=root, target_id=peer, kind='calls')` (root calls peer); supertypes = `relations(source_id=root, target_id=supertype, kind='inherits')` (root extends/implements supertype). Document the direction semantics in the rustdoc per-function and assert it in every test (SQL join on name + direction assertion).

`symbol_kind_to_entity_kind` mapping — keep it in a pure `const fn` (testable without SQLite). Map only the SymbolKinds Serena actually emits; everything else falls through to `"symbol"`. The `test_symbol_kind_mapping_covers_serena_emitted_kinds` test is the documentation-of-intent.

`tokio::time::timeout` discipline — inherit from `DiagnosticsClient` (WO-0015 landed `LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` = default 5000 ms wrap). Do NOT add a SECOND outer timeout — that would mask the typed `DiagnosticsClientError::Timeout` variant behind an opaque outer future (WO-0015 anti-pattern, documented in `quality_pipeline.rs` rustdoc).

Clippy pedantic + nursery are in effect (inherited from `lib.rs:15-17`) — expect `doc_markdown`, `missing_const_for_fn`, `missing_errors_doc`, `must_use_candidate`, `option_if_let_else`, `similar_names`. Fix, don't blanket-allow. The `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` at module scope is the ONE exception (error-type naming consistency).

Conventional Commits — every commit body ends with `Phase: 1 / Feature: P1-W5-F06 / Work-order: WO-0023`. Push after every commit. No `--amend` after push. No force-push. Target ~6 commits, ~800-1000 lines total (WO-0016 was +1023 lines / 5 files — this WO is similar size given 3 persist functions vs 1).

Session-abandonment prevention — before ending the session verify (a) at least one `.rs` diff against `origin/main` exists on the feature branch, (b) every commit has been pushed, AND (c) `0023-ready-for-review.md` has been written. If any is missing, either keep working or write an escalation explaining the blocker (WO-0012 anti-pattern — the RCA at `ucil-build/verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md` is the cautionary tale).

Reality-check survival (WO-0016/0017/0018/0019 pattern): `scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W5-F06` MUST pass on the delivered branch. Stashing the new `call_hierarchy.rs` module must cause the `call_hierarchy::` selector to fail (via compile error on the `lib.rs` `pub mod` line OR missing tests); popping must restore. If the script tripped on the `zero_tests=1` brand-new-module heuristic (WO-0016 escalation 20260415-1630), the verifier performs the manual two-step instead — document in the ready-for-review marker so the verifier doesn't re-flag.

commit_plan.subject: call_hierarchy: declare module + re-exports; add CallHierarchyError + uri_to_file_path + symbol_kind_to_entity_kind
